MASTER CALENDAR MOTION ON JUN-07-2022 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE. LAURA MAZZA, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA COURTCALL. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT CONTINUES THE HEARING TO JUN-14-2022 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206 FOR PLAINTIFF TO NOTIFY COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS, GREGORY WOOD, TO FILE A PROOF OF SERVICE ON ORDER GRANTING ATTORNEY'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FILED 1/3/22 UPON HIS CLIENTS. GREGORY WOOD, ESQ. IS STILL COUNSEL OF RECORD FOR DEFENDANTS. IF THERE IS NO PROOF OF SERVICE ON FILE, DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL MUST APPEAR AT THE HEARING. PLAINTIFF TO GIVE NOTICE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON JUN-14-2022 IN 206, PLAINTIFF JAMILA DASCO'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE WAS HEARD. LAURA MAZZA, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA COURTCALL. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS PER LOCAL RULE 2.7(B) AND REQUIRED PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PAY A $60 MOTION FILING FEE VIA E-FILING IN LIEU OF FILING A NEW MOTION. THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION. THE TRIAL DATE OF 08/08/22 IS CONTINUED TO 11/07/22 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. ALL DISCOVERY TO TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT CONFORMS WITH THE COURT'S RULING, APPROVED AS TO FORM, AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT. UPON APPROVAL AND FILING OF THE ORDER, MS. MAZZA TO SERVE ALL PARTIES. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; MATTER NOT REPORTED (206)

MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for notice of motion and motion for default judgment proveup hearing.

Dept. 514; Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 02:20:34 p.m. and concluding at 02:27:23 p.m.

Plaintiff Jamila Dasco appearing represented by Laura M. Mazza, is placed unde oath and testifies via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Westgate Renovators, Inc., a corporation not appearing.

The Court has reviewed the moving papers and recites its tentative ruling.
The matter is submitted.

Court awards attorney fees in the modified sum of $500 per hour + Enhancement.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents Set No One And Form Interrogatories Employment Set No One To Defendant Westgate Renovators Inc And Defendant Sinclair Painting Co Inc Corporate Defendants
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAMILA DASCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents Set No One And Form Interrogatories Employment Set No One To Defendant Westgate Renovators Inc And Defendant Sinclair Painting Co Inc Corporate Defendant

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED TO PLAINTIFF JAMILA DASCO'S (HEREAFTER "PLAINTIFF" OR "DASCO") MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS WESTGATE RENOVATORS, INC AND SINCLAIR PAINTING CO., INC. (HEREAFTER "CORPORATE DEFENDANTS") TO FURTHER RESPOND TO REQESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS SET ONE AND CERTAIN FORM INTERROGATORIES EMPLOYMENT SET ONE, ACCORDINGLY THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:

(1) CORPORATE DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES ARE OVERRULED. WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, CORPORATE DEFENDANTS WITH SERVE PLAINTIFF WITHOUT OBJECTIONS FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES SET ONE NOS. 201.6(a) & (c), 211.1, 214.1, 215.1, 215.2, 216.1 (b), (c), (d) & (f) .

(2) CORPORATE DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ARE OVERRULED. WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS CORPORATE DEFENDANTS WILL DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS. 1 -73 WITHOUT OBJECTION EXCEPT FOR A CLAIM OF ATTORNEY/CLIIENT PRIVILEGE. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANTS WITHHOLDS ANY DOCUMENT ON THE CLAIM OF A/C PRIVILEGE THEY WILL IDENTIFY EACH DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM.

(3) NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED ON ANY PARTY.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents Set No One And Form Interrogatories Employment Set No One To Defendant Mark Sinclair
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAMILA DASCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Requests For Production Of Documents Set No One And Form Interrogatories Employment Set No One To Defendant Mark Sinclair.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED TO PLAINTIFF JAMILA DASCO'S (HEREAFTER "PLAINTIFF" OR "DASCO") MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT MARK SINCLAIR (HEREAFTER "DEFENDANT" OF "SINCLAIR TO FURTHER RESPOND TO REQESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS SET ONE AND CERTAIN FORM INTERROGATORIES EMPLOYMENT SET ONE, ACCORDINGLY THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:

(1) SINCLAIR'S OBJECTIONS TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES ARE OVERRULED. WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER,SINCLAIR WITH SERVE PLAINTIFF WITHOUT OBJECTIONS FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES SET ONE NOS. 201.6, 211.1, 214.1, 215.1, 215.2, 216.1 (b) & (c).

(3) NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED ON ANY PARTY.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-20-2021 Continued To Nov-10-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Sinclair Painting Co., Inc., Westgate Renovators Inc., and Mark Sinclair's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied.

The arbitration agreement involved here is unenforceable. Labor Code section 432.6 provides that a person shall not, as a condition of employment, "require any applicant for employment or any employee to waive any right, forum, or procedure of the California Fair Employment and Housing Act . . . , including the right to file and pursue a civil action or a complaint with, or otherwise notify, . . . any court or other governmental entity of any alleged violation." (Lab. Code Sec. 432.6(a).) This section applies to contracts for employment entered into, modified, or extendfed on or after January 1, 2020. (Id. Sec. 432.6(h).) A court may award reasonable attorney's fees to a prevailing plaintiff enforcing their rights under section 432.6. (Id. Sec. 432.6(d).) It is an unlawful employment practice for an employer to violate section 432.6. (Gov. Code Sec. 12953.) Further, a violation of section 432.6 is a misdemeanor. (Lab. Code Sec. 433.)

Here, plaintiff was required as a condition of her employment on March 23, 2020 to sign the employment agreement containing the arbitration provision in question. (Dasco Decl. pars. 1, 2.) Labor Code section 432.6 was not "intended to invalidate a written arbitration agreement that is otherwise unenforceable under the Federal Arbitration Act." (Sec. 432.6(f).) However, defendant does not show or contend that the instant agreement is subject to the FAA. (See Carbajal v. CWPSC, Inc. (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 227, 237-241 [wage and hour claims under arbitration provision in employment agreement were not subject to FAA where moving party did not show that contract affected interstate commerce].) Section 432.6 therefore invalidates the arbitration agreement in plaintiff's employment agreement. (See also Chamber of Commerce of United States v. Bonta (9th Cir. Sept. 15, 2021), 2021 WL 4187860 [holding that Sec. 432.6 is not preempted by FAA].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
